content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 17,
1993
L.
Michael Bogert
Attorney at law
400 Capitol Mall,
Ste.
1800
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
1—93—092
Dear Mr.
Bogert:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice on behalf
of State Senator Frank Hill and his special election committee,
Citizens for Frank Hill—Senate 1990,
concerning the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
!
Your letter
states only a general question.
Therefore,
we consider it to be a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).
2
!
Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that campaign
debts incurred by a special election committee may not be
transferred to or assumed by a committee formed by the candidate
for another election.
In addition,
campaign debts incurred by a
special election committee must be retired with funds raised under
the contribution limitations set forth in Section 85305.
(Regulation 18535,
copy enclosed.)
1!
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


L
Michael Bogert
-
Our File No.
1—93-092
Page
2
If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
7ai
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure

sections.conclusion: Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that campaign debts incurred by a special election committee may not be transferred to or assumed by a committee formed by the candidate for another election. In addition, campaign debts incurred by a special election committee must be retired with funds raised under the contribution limitations set forth in Section 85305.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether campaign debts incurred by a special election committee may be transferred to or assumed by another committee, and how such debts must be retired.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign debts of a special election committee cannot be transferred to or assumed by a committee for another election and must be retired using funds raised subject to contribution limitations under Section 85305.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is FPPC confirmation of prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present, but conclusion appears in body paragraph. Classified as informal assistance under Regulation 18329(c). Footnotes indicate this informal advice does not provide full immunity protection.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether campaign debts incurred by a special election committee may be transferred to or assumed by another committee, and how such debts must be retired.

CONCLUSION: Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that campaign debts incurred by a special election committee may not be transferred to or assumed by a committee formed by the candidate for another election. In addition, campaign debts incurred by a special election committee must be retired with funds raised under the contribution limitations set forth in Section 85305.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission March 17, 1993 L. Michael Bogert Attorney at law 400 Capitol Mall, Ste. 1800 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. 1—93—092 Dear Mr. Bogert: You have requested confirmation of telephone advice on behalf of State Senator Frank Hill and his special election committee, Citizens for Frank Hill—Senate 1990, concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”). 1 ! Your letter states only a general question. Therefore, we consider it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c). 2 ! Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that campaign debts incurred by a special election committee may not be transferred to or assumed by a committee formed by the candidate for another election. In addition, campaign debts incurred by a special election committee must be retired with funds raised under the contribution limitations set forth in Section 85305. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. embedding.summary: FPPC confirms informal telephone advice provided to State Senator Frank Hill regarding campaign debt provisions, clarifying that special election committee debts cannot be transferred to other candidate committees and must be retired under specific contribution limitations.
